'''Scott Wilson''' (born 8 November 1970) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|fe}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Wilson as a youngster played in the South Sydney Juniors competition for [[Coogee Randwick Wombats]]. While at [[South Sydney High School]] in 1986, Wilson was selected to play for the [[Australia national schoolboy rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Sporting Pulse|title=Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Wilson made his first grade début for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in round 8 of the [[1988 NSWRL season]] against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] at [[Brookvale Oval]], coming off the bench in the second half. It later transpired that he was used as an illegal replacement and Souths were ultimately stripped of the two competition points they earned that afternoon. He would be sacked by South Sydney in 1990 after returning a position test for cocaine, and was sacked again by the [[North Sydney Bears]] during the 1991 season for another positive drugs test.<ref name=Redsplayers/>

He started at fullback in the [[1994 NSWRL season#Grand Final|1994 Winfield Cup Grand Final]] for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] against the [[Canberra Raiders]]. Wilson was benched by coach [[Chris Anderson (rugby)|Chris Anderson]] after a number of errors in the first half.<ref name=Redsplayers>{{cite web |last1=Elbra |first1=Tim |title=Death, drugs and dirty deeds: A brief, bizarre history of every Western Reds player |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-westernperth-reds-players-every-story-as-bears-brings-rugby-league-back-to-western-australia/news-story/08a33eddab3610177b83db46342ae19b |website=foxsports.com.au |publisher=News Corporation Australia |access-date=17 September 2025 |date=1 May 2025}}</ref>

Wilson played for six first grade clubs during his career: [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[North Sydney Bears]], {{nowrap|[[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]}} (twice), [[Gold Coast Chargers|Gold Coast]], [[WA Reds|Western Reds]] and [[North Queensland Cowboys]], scoring 30 tries in 109 first grade matches.<ref name=Redsplayers/> He also played for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Warrington Wolves]] in the Super League competition. 

Wilson was selected to represent New South Wales as an interchange player for game III of the [[Super League Tri-series|1997 Super League Tri-series]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Rugby League Project|title=Superleague Tri-Series 1997 Series - Game 3|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/matches/Superleague_Tri-Series_1997/Game_3/NSW_-SL--vs-New_Zealand.html|accessdate=2007-12-16|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080821022019/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/matches/Superleague_Tri-Series_1997/Game_3/NSW_-SL--vs-New_Zealand.html|archivedate=21 August 2008}}</ref>

==Career highlights==
* ''First Grade Debut:'' 1988 — Round 8, Rabbitohs vs [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] at [[Brookvale Oval]], 25 April<ref>{{cite web|publisher=NRL Stats|title=Scott Wilson|url=http://www.nrlstats.com/archive/players.cfm?PlayerID=357|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080729185636/http://www.nrlstats.com/archive/players.cfm?PlayerID=357|url-status=dead|archive-date=2008-07-29|accessdate=2007-12-16}}</ref>
* ''Representative Selection:'' 1997 — Game III, Super League Tri-series, NSW vs New Zealand at [[Bruce Stadium]], 14 May, winning 20–15
